title: Samsung SDI in Final Talks with Amazon AWS for $700M+ Data Center Battery Supply
detail: UPI and Korean media reported on April 28 that Samsung SDI and Amazon Web Services have entered final-stage negotiations for a battery backup unit (BBU) and UPS supply agreement valued at approximately $700 million to over 1 trillion Korean won. The deal would make Samsung SDI a key LFP battery supplier to the world's largest cloud infrastructure provider as AI-driven data center power demand surges globally. Samsung SDI confirmed talks are ongoing but that no contract has been finalized at the time of reporting. The potential agreement reflects a fast-growing data center battery market: hyperscalers require uninterruptible power for millions of servers, creating demand for large-format LFP cells with the cycle life, thermal stability, and cost economics suited to stationary standby applications. The deal, if finalized, would represent one of the largest data center battery supply contracts on record.

media.caption: Samsung SDI in final negotiations with Amazon AWS for data center battery backup supply worth $700M+

title: EU Commission Opens Consultation on Portable Battery Removability Exemptions
detail: The European Commission launched a four-week public consultation on April 28 proposing six new product-category exemptions from the EU Batteries Regulation's requirement that portable batteries in consumer devices be user-removable and replaceable by 2027. The removability requirement — part of the EU Battery Regulation (Regulation (EU) 2023/1542) — is designed to extend product lifetimes, reduce e-waste, and lower consumer repair costs. Stakeholders including manufacturers, NGOs, member-state governments, and consumer groups have until May 26 to respond. The exemptions cover specific product categories where technical integration makes removability impractical without fundamentally redesigning the product. The consultation represents a test of the EU's willingness to accommodate industry engineering realities while maintaining the regulation's core circular-economy objectives.

media.caption: EU Commission opens consultation on exemptions to the 2027 portable battery removability mandate
